lesson.menu_desc: Keeping a chat going with short answers & follow-up questions -- 28 slides
lesson.subtitle: Aula 15 -- Small Talk with Locals: Asking & Answering Personal Questions
lesson.title_tag: Professor View -- Roberto Pires | Aula 15 -- Small Talk with Locals

listenings.text: Hello there, and welcome to our little neighborhood. I am a local, and people here are very friendly, so do not be shy. The weather today is lovely, perfect for a walk. If you like a good view, the park nearby is wonderful in the morning. There is a small cafe on the corner where people love to chat. Where are you from? Is this your first time in our city? Feel free to ask me anything. I am always happy to help a visitor feel at home.

When I travel, I love small talk with the locals. At first I was a little shy, but now I always start with a simple question. I ask about the weather, the neighborhood, or a good place nearby. The trick is to listen and then ask a follow-up question, like what about you. People love to chat about their hometown, so I just let them talk. Last week a friendly woman told me she was married and lived near a beautiful view. A short, kind chat can turn a stranger into a friend.

extra_audio.text: First, Roberto smiles and says hello to a friendly local on the street. Then he starts with an easy question about the weather. Next, the local answers and asks Roberto where he is from. After that, they chat about the neighborhood and a nice view nearby. Finally, Roberto thanks the local, and they say goodbye like old friends.
